libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties appear required. The 2.0.0 API differs from 1.9.1: RenderingContext and SkinResourceLoader are gone; use DocumentRenderingContext and the current SiteRenderingContext API. DefaultSiteRenderer has injected collaborators, so meaningful render/locate coverage should use component-style setup or otherwise initialize collaborators rather than assuming direct construction covers the rendering path.

deterministic_setup.reason: doxia-site-renderer declares maven-artifact as provided, but public skin/context APIs use org.apache.maven.artifact.Artifact and DefaultArtifact; Gradle tests will not receive it transitively.
upstream 2.0.0 notes Doxia modules are no longer in doxia-core and must be stated explicitly so parser component descriptors are available for rendering APT site sources.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 1.9.1 tests are API-incompatible with 2.0.0 and must be rewritten rather than mechanically version-bumped.
If tests cover additional source formats such as xdoc or fml, matching doxia-module-xdoc or doxia-module-fml test dependencies may also be needed.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Doxia Site Renderer 2.0.0 exposes Maven Artifact skin APIs via a provided dependency and no longer bundles several Doxia parser modules needed to exercise normal site-source rendering.
